feedingProfile.preferredNpk: high-nitrogen (e.g. 10-10-10 base with nitrogen side-dress such as 15-0-0)

commonIssues: Bolting (premature seed-stalk formation) and bitter flavor when temperatures exceed about 24 C / 75 F or under long, hot, high-light days.
Shallow roots dry out quickly in containers; keep soil evenly moist and water more often than in-ground plantings (every 4-5 days, more in heat).
Pests: aphids, cabbage looper, corn earworm, leafhoppers, slugs and snails, cutworms, flea beetles.
Diseases: gray mold (Botrytis), Rhizoctonia bottom rot, Sclerotinia drop, aster yellows (spread by leafhoppers).
Tolerates light frost (to roughly -2 C / 28 F with cover) but freezing temperatures stop growth; protect with row cover in cold snaps.

source.citation: UMN Extension - Growing lettuce, endive and radicchio in home gardens. Optimal night low 50 F / day high 68 F; freezing stops growth; ~1 inch rainfall per week; 10-10-10 at planting plus nitrogen side-dress at 4 in tall; bolting when temps exceed ~75 F; pests include cutworms, slugs, flea beetles, aster yellows.
UMN Extension - Growing lettuce, endive and radicchio in home gardens. Spring planting in April when soil workable; fall ~3 months before first frost; productive cool-season window.
UMN Extension - Growing lettuce, endive and radicchio in home gardens. ~1 inch rainfall per week adequate; sandy soils need more frequent watering; water before harvest for crispness.

Clemson HGIC - Lettuce. Optimum growth 55-65 F; germination 60-80 F; light frost tolerated, ~4 degrees frost protection down to 28 F under cover; bolting/bitterness above 75 F; pre-plant 5-10-10 plus calcium-nitrate side-dress.

Clemson HGIC - Lettuce. Maintain uniform moisture, moisten soil to 6 inches deep; pests aphids, cabbage looper, corn earworm, leafhoppers, slugs, snails; diseases gray mold, Rhizoctonia bottom rot, Sclerotinia drop.
UGA Extension - Growing Lettuce in Your Garden. At least 8-10 hours of sunlight per day; slightly acidic soil pH 6.0-6.5; transplants 10-12 in apart.
UGA Extension - Growing Lettuce in Your Garden. Irrigate every 4-5 days as needed; container/raised-bed lettuce needs more frequent irrigation.
UGA Extension - Growing Lettuce in Your Garden. 5-10-10 at planting, then ~2 lb of 5-10-15 per 100 sq ft each month during the growing season (monthly feeding cadence).

UC ANR Master Gardeners (Under the Solano Sun) - Growing Lettuce in Containers. Container depth 6-9 inches suits shallow-rooted lettuce; bigger container gives more options; potting mix ~70-80% peat/coir, 20-30% perlite/bark, with dolomitic lime; high-nitrogen slow-release at planting plus liquid feed; full sun in spring, afternoon shade in heat.

Confidence note: Container volume in liters is estimated. Sources give container depth (UC ANR: 6-9 in) and per-plant footprint (an 8-inch / 18-inch pot per the search summary) rather than a liter figure; recommendedMinLiters 6 (single plant), idealMin 10, idealMax 40 are conservative conversions from those dimensions. wateringProfile baseIntervalDays 2 is tuned for shallow-rooted container culture (sources state 'every 4-5 days' in-ground and 'more frequent' in containers); hotWeatherMultiplier 0.6 and rainSkipThresholdMm 10 are app-tuning estimates derived from the ~1 in/week guidance. verticalSuitability 0.6 reflects suitability for shallow raised beds/window boxes, not a sourced rating.
